title: Savory French Salmon Terrine
description: Herbal, velvety French salmon terrine with pickled cucumber garnish, ideal for a sick day.
instructions: Place salmon fillet in a saucepan and cover with water; bring to a gentle simmer and poach for 8 minutes until just cooked.
Remove salmon and let cool, then flake it into small pieces, discarding any skin or bones.
In a medium saucepan, melt butter over low heat, add flour, and whisk until smooth to create a roux; cook for 1 minute.
Gradually pour in milk while whisking continuously until the sauce thickens and becomes silky.
Stir in egg yolk, lemon zest, lemon juice, herbs, and season with salt and pepper until fully incorporated.
Fold the flaked salmon into the sauce, ensuring an even distribution of fish and herbs.
Transfer the mixture to a lined terrine mold, smoothing the top with a spatula.
Bake in a preheated oven at 160 C for 25 minutes, or until set and lightly golden.
Cool the terrine to room temperature, then cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours to set completely.
Before serving, top the terrine with sliced pickled cucumber and a drizzle of lemony cream sauce, if desired.
